query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "reproducing, analyzing, and detecting reward hacking in rubric-based r" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by addressing the core mechanism of "reward hacking" and "rubric-based" reward specification (via reward machines) in the presence of noise, which is the fundamental challenge the user's follow-up research on detecting and analyzing reward hacking seeks to extend. It also fits criterion (d) as a foundational methodology paper for handling complex, sequence-dependent reward structures in RL, a necessary context for any study on reward hacking in rubric-based sys
